input.command: kubectl -n mongodb get configmap health-check-script -o jsonpath='{.data.probe\.sh}'

content.text: The probe script sleeps 2 seconds before even connecting, but the live StatefulSet has `timeoutSeconds: 1` and `failureThreshold: 1` on the readiness probe — every single probe run times out and instantly flips the pod to NotReady. This matches the "external automation" tampering the task warned about. The `kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration` annotation still shows the original sane values (readiness: timeout 5s/failureThreshold 6/period 5s; liveness: timeout 5s/failureThreshold 10/initialDelay 120s), so I'll restore those.

content.text: I already have the rollout status command running in the background (task `bvvu0mhkp`); I'll wait for its notification rather than polling.

input.description: wait for mongodb-replica rollout to finish
input.command: while kill -0 $(pgrep -f "rollout status statefulset/mongodb-replica" | head -1) 2>/dev/null; do sleep 2; done; echo done_waiting; kubectl -n mongodb get pods; kubectl -n mongodb rollout status statefulset/mongodb-replica --timeout=5s 2>&1 | tail -5

description: wait for mongodb-replica rollout to finish
